message:
chore(beads): close ft-tczj7 + file wa-nu4.3.9.19 follow-up

ft-tczj7 fixed in 987173cb; follow-up for live-slip discovery vs dead
branch removal filed as wa-nu4.3.9.19.

Agent: RusticMeadow

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-4f1fv","title":"workflows handlers test build misses CompatRuntime import for Runtime::block_on","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:10:03.547663Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:08:14.479058Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:08:14.478891Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-4o5mb","title":"[conformance] wa.search / wa.get_text / wa.send / wa.wait_for lack schema+envelope golden roundtrip","description":"# Gap

`crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_conformance.rs` pins envelope + schema conformance for exactly ONE tool: `wa.rules_list` (tests at lines 237-294, 313-346). `conformance_mcp_manifest.rs` pins the MANIFEST (tool schemas, additionalProperties=false, resource templates) but does NOT exercise the per-tool request → response envelope roundtrip.

`grep -oE '"wa\.[a-z_]+"' crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_conformance.rs | sort -u` → only `"wa.rules_list"`.

Out of 29 advertised `wa.*` tools in `tests/fixtures/mcp_manifest.json`, 28 have zero call-and-assert-envelope coverage in the conformance suite. The four core operator-facing tools are:

* `wa.search` — JSON envelope shape, hit schema, TOON parity, pane_filter/since_filter/until_filter/mode/metrics fields
* `wa.get_text` — pane_text map shape (ok/error variants), truncated + truncation_info edges
* `wa.send` — sender/response envelope (distinct from get_text), error codes for denied sends
* `wa.wait_for` — matched/elapsed_ms/polls/is_regex fields, timeout response envelope, rule_id match path

## Shape of fix

Mirror the `mcp_conformance_rules_list_*` test pattern (lines 237-294 in mcp_conformance.rs):

1. spawn_client() → call_tool("wa.search", …)
2. parse_tool_envelope() → assert_success_envelope_shape() → assert_*_data_matches_documented_schema()
3. Also pin: invalid-format rejection, invalid-args hint text, TOON parity where applicable.

Existing helpers (`assert_object_matches_documented_schema`, `assert_common_envelope_fields`, `assert_success_envelope_shape`, `assert_invalid_args_envelope_shape`) already generalise — the new tests should reuse them verbatim.

## Why this matters

The MCP manifest conformance is the FREEZE on the advertised schema. Without per-tool envelope tests, a refactor can pass `conformance_mcp_manifest` (schema still looks right) while silently returning a non-conforming response body — e.g. field renamed in `ScannerHit` → clients break but tests stay green. ft-bs0ec froze this for `agent_inventory`; the four core operator tools deserve the same treatment.

## Scope guard

One new test file `mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs` (or additions to `mcp_conformance.rs`) with 4× (success + invalid-args) = 8 tests. ~250 LOC. No production changes.

## Explicitly out of scope

Mission / tx / events / cass families (separate bead if needed). Manifest tests (already covered). Resource templates (already covered).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T05:49:57.026144Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:46:38.216319Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3608,"issue_id":"ft-4o5mb","author":"jemanuel","text":"Blocker (pane3, 2026-04-23): fastmcp-rust enforces tool input_schema type validation at the JSON-RPC boundary — wrong-typed required fields (e.g. pane_id string where integer required) return McpError::InvalidParams from call_tool BEFORE reaching the per-tool handler's FT-MCP-0001 envelope path. The pattern mcp_conformance.rs:280 uses for wa.rules_list (optional string agent_type) cannot be replayed for wa.search/get_text/send/wait_for via wrong-typed-required inputs. Next-hop approach: pin the SUCCESS envelope shape via {timeout_secs:1, pane_id:999, pattern:'never-matches', format:'json'} for wait_for (times out in ~1s, returns Ok envelope with matched:false/elapsed_ms/polls). Draft tests left UNTRACKED at crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs — first pass covers invalid-args envelopes and doesn't work as noted. Bead stays in_progress for handoff.","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:27:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-4o5mb.1","title":"[conformance] wa.state lacks schema+envelope golden roundtrip","description":"Add isolated MCP conformance coverage for wa.state in its own test file, using the golden-capture helper pattern and manifest schema freeze. Cover success and invalid-args envelopes without editing mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:30:32.760784Z","created_by":"LilacBay","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:50:51.812852Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-4o5mb.1","depends_on_id":"ft-4o5mb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:30:32.760784Z","created_by":"LilacBay","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3619,"issue_id":"ft-4o5mb.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"Blocked while landing wa.state conformance harness. Narrow lane used: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-MistyCoast-target CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test wa_state_mcp_conformance --features mcp,asupersync-runtime. That fell back locally on RCH-E324, then local wrapper reproduced a compile-stop before the new test ran: crates/frankenterm-core/src/event_stream.rs has non-exhaustive matches for Err(events::RecvError::Cancelled) at lines 574, 586, 749, and 786 (E0004). I stayed inside the assigned slice; only new file is crates/frankenterm-core/tests/wa_state_mcp_conformance.rs and it remains uncommitted pending baseline compile repair.","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:50:51Z"}]}
-{"id":"ft-4o5mb.2","title":"[conformance] wa.events lacks schema+envelope golden roundtrip","description":"Create a dedicated MCP conformance test file for wa.events using the golden-capture helper pattern already used in mcp_conformance_mission_tx.rs. Cover one seeded-success envelope with stored event + annotations plus one invalid-args envelope via out-of-range limit. Reuse manifest schema freeze; do not touch mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs (pane 2 owns it).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:31:02.808965Z","created_by":"LilacBay","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:51:45.680055Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:51:45.679776Z","close_reason":"Added isolated wa.events MCP conformance harness in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/wa_events_mcp_conformance.rs; local cargo-local --no-run is blocked by current dirty runtime_compat RecvError::Cancelled fallout in crates/frankenterm-core/src/events.rs and crates/frankenterm-core/src/workflows/runner.rs, and rch workers are still incompatible with required CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang on Linux.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-4o5mb.2","depends_on_id":"ft-4o5mb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:31:02.808965Z","created_by":"LilacBay","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Reuse manifest schema freeze; do not touch mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs (pane 2 owns it).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:31:02.808965Z","created_by":"LilacBay","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:59:37.706900Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:59:37.706467Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 01d5e233","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-4o5mb.2","depends_on_id":"ft-4o5mb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:31:02.808965Z","created_by":"LilacBay","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3620,"issue_id":"ft-4o5mb.2","author":"jemanuel","text":"Reopened: Restamping close reason to shipped commit per follow-up","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:59:33Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-4o5mb.3","title":"[conformance] wa.reserve / wa.reservations / wa.release lack envelope coverage","description":"Add a dedicated MCP conformance test file for the reservation tool family. Use the mission/tx golden-capture pattern, seed storage directly, and pin success plus invalid-args envelopes for wa.reserve, wa.reservations, and wa.release without touching mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:36:28.848013Z","created_by":"LilacBay","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:36:28.848013Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-4o5mb.3","depends_on_id":"ft-4o5mb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:36:28.848013Z","created_by":"LilacBay","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-4qom2","title":"[SEV] tmux cmd_queue cap only enforced at pop site; unbounded growth when state != Idle","description":"frankenterm/mux/src/tmux.rs TmuxDomainState.cmd_queue has CMD_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH=50_000 enforced only inside send_next_command at line 289 via cmd_queue.drain(..excess). send_next_command early-returns at line 286-288 when state != Idle, so the drain-oldest logic never runs if tmux is stuck in State::WaitingForResponse.

Four push sites append to cmd_queue with NO cap check:
- line 196: Event::LayoutChange -> ListAllPanes
- line 223: Event::SessionChanged -> ListCommands
- line 234: Event::WindowAdd -> ListAllWindows
- line 375: split_pane (user split) -> SplitPane

Concrete interleaving: (1) tmux sends a command, enters State::WaitingForResponse; (2) tmux server is slow/hung (real-world scenario: remote tmux on lagging SSH link, tmux server overloaded, Event::Guarded response delayed); (3) GUI user resizes window repeatedly -> stream of Event::LayoutChange -> each push_back at line 196 runs without cap check; (4) send_next_command early-returns because state != Idle. Queue grows unbounded -> memory leak, eventual OOM.

The CMD_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH comment at line 32-33 explicitly advertises 'prevent unbounded memory growth during event storms' as a safety invariant. That invariant is currently only upheld at the pop site, where it can't run under the exact condition (stuck tmux) that event storms exploit.

Fix: extract the cap-enforcement logic into a push_command_capped helper that applies the drain-oldest + warn/error logging at every push site. The helper runs under the cmd_queue lock (just acquired by the caller), so it's atomic with the push and has no additional locking cost.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T00:17:26.601099Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T00:21:03.160715Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T00:21:03.158111Z","close_reason":"Completed: added push_command_capped() helper and routed all 4 production push sites (LayoutChange/SessionChanged/WindowAdd/split_pane) through it. The hard cap is now enforced at push time, not only at pop time — closing the unbounded-growth window when tmux is stuck in State::WaitingForResponse. cargo check passes clean. Commit 1055cffc.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-4z7vh","title":"RobotCommands::Workflow builds PolicyEngine without require_prompt_active/command_gate/rules — bypasses operator safety config","description":"AUTH BYPASS (robot mode mutations, Severity: Medium).

crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs has 10 PolicyEngine::new call sites. 8 of them wire require_prompt_active from config + .with_command_gate_config(config.safety.command_gate.clone()) + .with_policy_rules(config.safety.rules.clone()). Two do NOT, both under RobotCommands::Workflow:

Site 1 (main.rs:19831, RobotCommands::Workflow::Run):

  let policy_engine = PolicyEngine::new(
      config.safety.rate_limit_per_pane,
      config.safety.rate_limit_global,
      false, // Don't require prompt active for robot mode
  )
  .with_tuning(&config.tuning);

No command_gate, no policy_rules, and require_prompt_active hardcoded false. This engine is fed into CxPolicyInjector → WorkflowRunner; any workflow step that calls send_text (e.g., handle_compaction sending '/compact', handle_usage_limit sending '/refresh') bypasses:
  - the operator's safety.require_prompt_active=true policy,
  - every [[safety.rules]] custom gate,
  - the [safety.command_gate] allow/deny list.

Site 2 (main.rs:20511, RobotCommands::Workflow::Abort): identical construction. abort_execution_with_cx runs compensation steps through the same injector; any send_text in a compensation path bypasses the same three surfaces.

Compare the correctly-gated paths:
  - RobotCommands::Send at main.rs:17652 uses config.safety.require_prompt_active AND .with_command_gate_config + .with_policy_rules.
  - Non-robot workflow invocations at main.rs:21361, 21533, 25051, 27347 all wire the full policy surface.

NOT a duplicate of ft-48ah6 (read-only policy): this bead is specifically about the write/mutation surface (workflow's send_text / command-bearing steps). ft-48ah6 covered the read/search side.

Fix: replace the hardcoded 'false' with config.safety.require_prompt_active at both sites, and chain .with_command_gate_config(config.safety.command_gate.clone()) + .with_policy_rules(config.safety.rules.clone()) before passing the engine to PolicyGatedInjector. Rate-limit args are already correct.

Regression test: a send inside a robot-invoked workflow must be DENIED when config.safety.require_prompt_active=true AND no prompt is active, matching the direct-send gate behavior. Currently it is allowed.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane5","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:46:59.925741Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T07:19:54.084786Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T07:19:54.084480Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 834b59f2. Both RobotCommands::Workflow::Run (main.rs:19831) and ::Abort (main.rs:20520) now build PolicyEngine with config.safety.require_prompt_active and chain .with_command_gate_config + .with_policy_rules — matching the construction at RobotCommands::Send (main.rs:17652) and non-robot workflow sites (21361/21533/25051/27347). Regression test robot_workflow_policy_engine_denies_send_when_prompt_required_ft_4z7vh asserts SendText is denied when require_prompt_active=true + command_running. Test binary compile is currently blocked by pre-existing concurrent-agent breakage (E0603 macro select private, E0063 missing defer_fts_triggers field) unrelated to this fix.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
@@ -284,7 +284,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-t4n0z","title":"[fuzz] add termwiz::input::InputParser::parse fuzz target for raw keystroke bytes","description":"Surface: frankenterm/termwiz/src/input.rs:1454 — pub fn parse(&mut self, bytes: &[u8], callback, maybe_more). Parses raw keystroke ANSI escape sequences (arrow keys, function keys, mouse events, CSI u keys, bracketed paste, OSC responses) from stdin. Directly accepts untrusted byte streams via &[u8]; no existing fuzz target covers it (termwiz_csi_parser is a different stage — output CSI — and escape_parser_raw covers terminal output parsing, not keyboard input).

Proposed harness: fuzz/fuzz_targets/termwiz_input_parser.rs
  #![no_main]
  use libfuzzer_sys::fuzz_target;
  use termwiz::input::InputParser;
  fuzz_target!(|data: &[u8]| {
      if data.len() > 1_000_000 { return; }
      let mut parser = InputParser::new();
      let _ = parser.parse_as_vec(data, false);
      let _ = parser.parse_as_vec(data, true);  // maybe_more=true path
  });

Corpus seeds: fuzz/corpus/termwiz_input_parser/ — minimal valid sequences from existing termwiz tests (input.rs:1482-1540 fixtures): empty, bare ESC, CSI cursor keys, CSI u keyboard, SS3 function keys, OSC 52 clipboard response, bracketed paste markers, bracket mouse reports (SGR and legacy).

Add to fuzz/Cargo.toml [[bin]] with the termwiz path dependency already present.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-23T05:49:04.832273Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T06:00:04.935376Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T06:00:04.934979Z","close_reason":"Added fuzz/fuzz_targets/termwiz_input_parser.rs + 25 corpus seeds; cargo fuzz build termwiz_input_parser succeeds","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-t62hq","title":"[SEV] mcp wa.wait_for + wa.send accept timeout_secs=0 despite schema minimum:1 — silent zero-duration wait","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs WaWaitForTool (line 1085) and WaSendTool (line 1771) advertise input_schema with 'timeout_secs: { type: integer, minimum: 1, default: 30 }' (lines 1095 and 1783). WaitForParams.timeout_secs and SendParams.timeout_secs are both u64 in mcp_types.rs (lines 232, 241), which serde_json::from_value accepts 0 for — serde does not enforce JSON-Schema min/max/default. No post-parse bounds check exists at either call site.

Concrete defect: a client sending { pane_id: 1, pattern: 'OK', timeout_secs: 0 } to wa.wait_for hits line 1166 'let timeout = std::time::Duration::from_secs(timeout_secs);' with timeout = 0. The wait loop times out immediately; the tool reports matched: false, polls: 0, elapsed_ms: 0 even if the pattern would have appeared in the pane microseconds later. Same gap in wa.send at line 1928. Clients that type-coerce (e.g., JS Math.floor(0.5) = 0, CLI timeout=0 as 'no timeout') land on this silently.

Precedent: the wa.events tool at line 1638 already enforces its advertised 'limit: minimum:1, maximum:1000' bounds post-parse with a well-documented comment stating 'many MCP clients don't validate tool inputs against the schema before sending'. That comment applies equally to timeout_secs.

Fix: add the same post-parse validation for timeout_secs at both call sites, returning MCP_ERR_INVALID_ARGS with a message pointing at the schema's minimum. Pattern matches the wa.events enforcement block exactly.

Also note (tracked separately if filed): wa.cass_search, wa.cass_view, wa.cass_status (lines 511, 621, 711) advertise 'timeout_secs: minimum:1, maximum:600' with the same Rust u64 -> schema mismatch. They pass params.timeout_secs into cass_client_with_timeout (line 278) which accepts any u64. This bead scopes to wa.wait_for + wa.send only to keep the fix focused.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T00:25:59.494378Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T00:29:38.622911Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T00:29:38.622531Z","close_reason":"Completed: added post-parse timeout_secs>=1 validation at WaWaitForTool.call and WaSendTool.call matching the wa.events limit-enforcement pattern (line 1638). Clients sending timeout_secs=0 now receive MCP_ERR_INVALID_ARGS with a schema-referencing hint instead of a silent zero-duration no-op. cass tools (lines 511/621/711) have the same gap, scoped out. Commit 8dc7dbde.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-t995m","title":"escape-parser: tmux_cc feature build broken (130 compile errors) — proptest macro expansion E0277 on Vec<usize>/String strategies","description":"cargo test -p frankenterm-escape-parser --lib --features tmux_cc fails with 130 compile errors. All from the existing proptest block at tmux_cc/mod.rs:1397-1409 (parser_events_are_chunk_boundary_invariant, added by 3abc11d2). Error shape: E0277 'the size for values of type [usize] cannot be known at compilation time' inside proptest::sugar::NamedArguments<N, V> — the macro is trying to use slice-unsized V where it should be Vec-sized. Also 'str' / '[usize]' sized errors with the same root cause. Looks like a proptest 1.11 macro-expansion breakage or a version mismatch between the workspace pin (1.11) and the crate's dev-dep pin (1.8). The tmux_cc feature tests never run in CI when this is broken — my ft-kfy99 fix is latent until this unblocks. Scope: this bead only tracks the test-build breakage; the underlying logic change in ft-kfy99 is unaffected.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:41:05.738611Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:22:12.427122Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:22:12.426873Z","close_reason":"Landed in 03bfed4c (fix(escape-parser): tmux_cc feature requires std). Cargo.toml tmux_cc feature now chains 'std' so the module's HashMap/String/ToString/format! usage resolves. cargo check -p frankenterm-escape-parser --tests --features tmux_cc now builds clean (3.86s); pre-fix it produced 90 compile errors. Unblocks ft-kfy99's regression test + parser_events_are_chunk_boundary_invariant.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-tczj7","title":"[review][LOW] ecae6caa rewrite drops FT-MCP-0001 tool-level serde-reject coverage, replaces with framework InvalidParams only","description":"The pre-commit mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs (ft-4o5mb first pass) exercised the TOOL-level serde rejection path: call_tool with wrong-typed args like `{"query": 42}` would reach the tool handler at crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs:1387-1399 where `serde_json::from_value::<SearchParams>` failed and emitted the documented MCP_ERR_INVALID_ARGS (aka FT-MCP-0001) envelope.

ecae6caa replaced that with `{"query": "needle", "limit": 0}` — which triggers FRAMEWORK-level schema validation (limit minimum:1) and returns `FrameworkMcpError { code: InvalidParams }`, serialized by parse_invalid_args_response as `{"kind": "framework_error", "code": "InvalidParams", ...}`. The 4 tests now assert `assert_framework_invalid_params_response(...)` with the framework error shape, NOT the tool envelope shape.

Effect: the FT-MCP-0001 tool-level error contract is no longer fenced by any test in this file. A refactor that deletes or renames the serde-reject branch at mcp_tools.rs:1387-1399, 1392-1396, etc. — or that changes the hint strings quoted in the original bead ft-4o5mb description — passes these tests silently.

Scope question: is FT-MCP-0001 still meant to be part of the public MCP contract, or has the framework schema validation now superseded it? If superseded, mcp_tools.rs:1387-1399 and the parallel branches in wa.get_text/wa.send/wa.wait_for are dead code and should be removed. If not superseded, we need one regression test per tool that slips past schema validation into the serde-reject path.

Easiest slip path: a field whose JSON-schema type is `integer` accepts `0` through bounds validation but fails at serde deserialization because the tool struct uses `NonZeroU64`. Not the case today (tool structs use plain u64), so the only reliable way to hit serde rejection is with wrong-typed args — which framework may or may not catch depending on schema strictness for each tool.

Fix path: one regression test per tool sending args that pass schema validation but fail serde. Concrete candidate: send an array as a string field, or malform a nested object that additionalProperties:false doesn't check recursively. Verify FT-MCP-0001 still surfaces.

Severity: LOW — not a regression in production behavior, just a coverage regression. Documented so a future ft-4o5mb-follow-up bead can pick it up.

Tag: review, mcp, conformance, coverage. Audit round 7, 2026-04-23, verifying ecae6caa.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:43:47.415916Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:52:08.211611Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
+{"id":"ft-tczj7","title":"[review][LOW] ecae6caa rewrite drops FT-MCP-0001 tool-level serde-reject coverage, replaces with framework InvalidParams only","description":"The pre-commit mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs (ft-4o5mb first pass) exercised the TOOL-level serde rejection path: call_tool with wrong-typed args like `{"query": 42}` would reach the tool handler at crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs:1387-1399 where `serde_json::from_value::<SearchParams>` failed and emitted the documented MCP_ERR_INVALID_ARGS (aka FT-MCP-0001) envelope.

ecae6caa replaced that with `{"query": "needle", "limit": 0}` — which triggers FRAMEWORK-level schema validation (limit minimum:1) and returns `FrameworkMcpError { code: InvalidParams }`, serialized by parse_invalid_args_response as `{"kind": "framework_error", "code": "InvalidParams", ...}`. The 4 tests now assert `assert_framework_invalid_params_response(...)` with the framework error shape, NOT the tool envelope shape.

Effect: the FT-MCP-0001 tool-level error contract is no longer fenced by any test in this file. A refactor that deletes or renames the serde-reject branch at mcp_tools.rs:1387-1399, 1392-1396, etc. — or that changes the hint strings quoted in the original bead ft-4o5mb description — passes these tests silently.

Scope question: is FT-MCP-0001 still meant to be part of the public MCP contract, or has the framework schema validation now superseded it? If superseded, mcp_tools.rs:1387-1399 and the parallel branches in wa.get_text/wa.send/wa.wait_for are dead code and should be removed. If not superseded, we need one regression test per tool that slips past schema validation into the serde-reject path.

Easiest slip path: a field whose JSON-schema type is `integer` accepts `0` through bounds validation but fails at serde deserialization because the tool struct uses `NonZeroU64`. Not the case today (tool structs use plain u64), so the only reliable way to hit serde rejection is with wrong-typed args — which framework may or may not catch depending on schema strictness for each tool.

Fix path: one regression test per tool sending args that pass schema validation but fail serde. Concrete candidate: send an array as a string field, or malform a nested object that additionalProperties:false doesn't check recursively. Verify FT-MCP-0001 still surfaces.

Severity: LOW — not a regression in production behavior, just a coverage regression. Documented so a future ft-4o5mb-follow-up bead can pick it up.

Tag: review, mcp, conformance, coverage.  {"id":"ft-trby3","title":"[reality-gap] RecorderBackendKind::FrankenSqlite is enum-reachable but runtime-unreachable","description":"# Claim (README)

README.md:62 lists "**Replay & Forensics**: Capture, replay, and
diff decision graphs for post-incident analysis and regression
testing" as a core feature. No distinction between backends is
offered — the user's mental model is "replay just works."

# Reality (code)

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/recorder_storage.rs:560-578`:

```rust
match config.backend {
    RecorderBackendKind::AppendLog => { ...opens storage... }
    RecorderBackendKind::FrankenSqlite => Err(
        RecorderStorageError::BackendUnavailable {
            backend: RecorderBackendKind::FrankenSqlite,
            message: "frankensqlite backend not yet implemented".to_string(),
        }
    ),
}
```

The enum variant is public, serde-deserializable (confirmed by
presence on `RecorderStorageConfig.backend` field), and reachable
from any operator who writes `backend = "frankensqlite"` (or
whatever serde rename applies) in their ft.toml's recorder section.

# Failure shape

Operator configures frankensqlite → app bootstraps → recorder
bootstrap returns `BackendUnavailable` → app either:
  (a) aborts with the "not yet implemented" message (informative);
  (b) silently falls back to AppendLog (if caller does);
  (c) propagates the error and the whole watch daemon fails to start.

Which branch fires depends on callers of `bootstrap_recorder_storage`
— worth verifying in a follow-up. Best case (a) wastes operator's
time; worst case (c) is a surprise crash.

# Recommended disposition

Either:
  (a) Remove `FrankenSqlite` from the serde-exposed enum until the
      backend is actually implemented. Zero blast radius — the
      variant is only reachable by operator opt-in today.
  (b) Keep the variant but add a deprecation/preview flag that
      operators must explicitly set (e.g., `experimental_backend =
      true`) so accidental selection is impossible.
  (c) Implement the FrankenSqlite backend.

(a) or (b) ship in minutes; (c) is a subsystem-sized undertaking.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-21T20:44:30.571102Z","created_by":"reality-check","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:18:55.998924Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:51:20.514877Z","close_reason":"README + flight-recorder docs now disclose that append_log is the only shipped recorder backend and frankensqlite remains rollout/test-only until bootstrap support lands.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["reality-gap","recorder","storage"]}
 {"id":"ft-trot7","title":"telemetry::Histogram::record poisons total_sum/min/max on f64::INFINITY (NaN guard is too narrow)","description":"## Defect

\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/telemetry.rs:339\` — \`Histogram::record\`
guards against NaN but does **not** guard against \`f64::INFINITY\` or
\`f64::NEG_INFINITY\`. The recently-added [ft-b4l62] guard catches NaN
because NaN poisons \`partial_cmp\` and the running sum, but Inf has the
same poisoning shape and slips through unflagged.

## Concrete failure path

1. Caller invokes \`Histogram::record(f64::INFINITY)\`. Easy upstream
   trigger: any latency calculation of the form
   \`numerator / denom_seconds\` where \`denom_seconds == 0.0\` (clock
   skew, mocked time fixture, division-before-clamp bug). Or a
   subtraction of two timestamps that overflowed to Inf in a longer
   pipeline.
2. Line 340 \`if value.is_nan() { return; }\` — Inf is not NaN, falls
   through to mutate state.
3. Line 344 \`self.total_sum += value\`. \`f64 + INF = INF\`. \`total_sum\`
   is now \`INF\` permanently — every subsequent finite sample preserves
   the INF (\`INF + finite = INF\`).
4. Line 348-349 \`if value > self.max { self.max = value }\`. Initial
   \`max = NEG_INFINITY\` so \`INF > NEG_INFINITY\` is true → \`max = INF\`.
   Every later finite value compares \`finite > INF\` → false, so \`max\`
   stays at INF forever.
5. \`Histogram::mean()\` (line 401) returns \`INF / count = INF\`.
6. \`Histogram::summary()\` returns \`Some(INF)\` for \`mean\`, \`max\`, and
   potentially \`p99\`. \`HistogramSummary\` is \`Serialize\`; serde_json's
   default serializer emits \`null\` for non-finite f64 (RFC 8259 forbids
   Inf in JSON), silently dropping the value from telemetry payloads
   that downstream dashboards consume.
7. Symmetric for \`record(f64::NEG_INFINITY)\` — \`total_sum\` and \`min\`
   become NEG_INFINITY permanently, mean reports \`-INF\`.

## Severity rationale

- Same poisoning shape as ft-b4l62 (NaN), ft-761tz (NaN in
  disk_pressure), ft-yskcu (NaN in continuous_backpressure), ft-7s0vw
  (overflow in event_id) — silent wrong answer with no self-recovery.
- Existing NaN guard makes the absence of an Inf guard surprising:
  reviewers reading the code see \`is_nan()\` and assume the histogram
  is hardened, when it is not.
- Reachable via legitimate division-by-zero in any client computing
  durations or rates as f64.

## Fix sketch

1. \`Histogram::record\` (line 340) — broaden the guard from
   \`if value.is_nan()\` to \`if !value.is_finite()\`. \`f64::is_finite\`
   is true iff the value is neither NaN nor ±Infinity.
2. Update the doc comment and \`[ft-b4l62]\` reference to note that the
   guard now covers Inf as well.
3. Regression tests covering: (a) record(INFINITY) is no-op,
   total_sum stays 0.0, mean stays None; (b) record(NEG_INFINITY) is
   no-op; (c) record(finite) then record(INFINITY) then record(finite)
   — second finite blends correctly without Inf contamination;
   (d) summary().max stays Some(finite_value) after an Inf attempt.

## Scope

\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/telemetry.rs\` only. No cross-crate API
changes. \`HistogramSummary\` shape unchanged.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane0","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:58:02.402142Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:06:14.413298Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T12:06:14.413032Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit f73e139a: Histogram::record now uses !value.is_finite() to reject both NaN and ±INFINITY. 4 regression tests added covering pos-Inf, neg-Inf, all-Inf, and summary().max stays finite after Inf attempt. All 74 histogram-related tests pass against /tmp/ft-rv-p0-target. Pattern matches the arithmetic-poison family alongside ft-b4l62 (NaN guard, original), ft-761tz, ft-yskcu, ft-7s0vw.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
